nameMeanings.name: Butler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from a word that originally denoted a wine steward, usually the chief servant of a medieval household, from Norman French butuiller (Old French bouteillier, Latin buticularius, from buticula ‘bottle’). In the large households of royalty and the most powerful nobility, the title came to denote an officer of high rank and responsibility, only nominally concerned with the supply of wine, if at all. As well as being widespread in England, this is also the surname of an important Irish family, descended from Theobald FitzWalter, who was appointed Chief Butler of Ireland by King Henry II in 1177. It is Gaelicized as de Buitléir.

English: occasionally perhaps an occupational name from Middle English boteler ‘maker of bottles (usually of leather)’, a derivative of Middle English botel, Old French bo(u)teille ‘bottle’ and synonymous with Botelmaker.
Americanized form of French   Bouthillier   (see   Bouteiller  ).

variants.name: Buttler
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Butler  .
German: occupational name for a village tavern owner, from French bouteillier ‘butler’.
Americanized form of German Buttlar: habitational name from a place so named in Thuringia.

variants.name: Bouthillier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Jacques, Normand, Philippe, Yves.
French: variant of   Bouteiller  . This name is sometimes Americanized as   Butler  . Compare   Boutilier  .

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

en.lifeSummary: When Alice Butler was born in 1406, in Lancaster, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, her father, John Butler, was 22 and her mother, Isabel Fleming, was 22. She married Richard De Clyfton Baron Clifton of Goosnarth about 1427, in Goosnargh, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in 1444, in Clayton le Moors, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 38, and was buried in Clayton le Moors, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom.

Elle est décédée en 1444, à Clayton le Moors, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 38 ans, et a été enterrée à Clayton le Moors, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: Alice Butler(이)가 1406년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 랭커스터에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Butler(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel Fleming(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 대략 1427년 영국 랭카실르 구스나르그h에서 Richard De Clyfton Baron Clifton of Goosnarth(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1444년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 클레이턴 일 무르스에서 38세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 클레이턴 일 무르스에 묻혔다.
